name: Machine Crunch
resistance_profile: Fixed-path spinal flexion against a selectorized stack — even, easily progressible resistance through the crunch, ideal for adding load.
cues: Round the spine to crunch, don't just hinge at the hips
Drive the ribs toward the pelvis
Control the return and resist the stack
execution_steps: Set the seat and pad so the movement pivots at your mid-torso, and select a load.
Sit tall, hook under the pads or grab the handles, and brace the abs.
Crunch by flexing the spine, driving your ribs toward your pelvis.
Squeeze at full flexion for a moment.
Return under control to a slight stretch without letting the stack yank you back.
good_when: You're a beginner who wants easy, safe progressive overload for the abs
You want to load spinal flexion without a cable setup
bad_when: The machine's pivot doesn't line up with your spine
You only have free weights or bands available
common_errors: Pulling with the arms or hip flexors instead of flexing the spine
Using momentum to fling through reps
progressions: Pause and squeeze at full flexion
